svg.text: Why AI Ad Spend Keeps Outrunning Proof
Four gaps stacking on top of each other, and the one fix that closes all of them

g.text: CMOs allocate 15.3% of marketing budgets to AI, but only 30% say their teams are ready to scale it.
2. The supply chain underneath has not changed
Only 36 cents of every programmatic dollar reaches the consumer. AI optimizes inside that ceiling, not around it.
3. The creative win is conditional, not automatic
A 16 billion impression study found AI generated ad images only out click human made ones when they do not look AI generated.
4. Governance is the slowest moving piece
Gartner expects 60% of brands to run agentic AI by 2028, while audit and measurement practices lag behind adoption.
The fix: prove it before you scale it
Close the gap by measuring each layer on its own terms: budget against readiness, creative against detection, and automation against an audit trail you control.

svg.text: Sources: Gartner, Forrester, ANA / Kroll, IAB, and the "AI in Disguise" academic study (Columbia, Harvard, TU Munich)
